title: Handoff bundle + announce-back provenance primitive
goal: Implement the handoff bundle (`MailboxHandoffBundleV1`) and announce-back provenance primitive per spec v02.186 §02-system-architecture.md role mailbox subsection [ADD v02.177]: when one executor hands work to another (delegate_work, escalation, takeover) it produces a typed bundle containing context, artifacts, transcript reference, and provenance hash. When the receiver completes, it produces an `announce_back` message carrying the bundle reference + completion artifacts + provenance chain.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/handoff.rs with `MailboxHandoffBundleV1`, `HandoffBundleBuilder`, `AnnounceBackComposer`; integration test under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_handoff_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-179 message families (DelegateWorkBody, AnnounceBackBody) merged; MT-178 exporter merged so handoff bundles serialize via the same projection path.
implementation_notes: Per spec [ADD v02.177] line 6160 (role mailbox handoff bundle, note transcription, announce-back provenance): define `MailboxHandoffBundleV1 { bundle_id: Uuid v7, source_thread_id: Uuid v7, source_message_id: Uuid v7, target_role: RoleId, target_executor_kind: ExecutorKind, context_summary: String, linked_artifacts: Vec<ArtifactPointer>, transcript_pointer: Option<TranscriptPointer>, capability_grants: Vec<CapabilityGrant>, expires_at_utc: Option, content_hash: String (sha256 of canonical JSON), created_at_utc, created_by_session: Uuid v7 }`. `HandoffBundleBuilder` is a typestate builder that requires every required field before `.build()` can be called. The bundle is stored in a new `role_mailbox_handoff_bundle` Postgres table with FKs to thread + message, and content_hash is recomputed on insert (validator enforced). `AnnounceBackComposer::compose(bundle_ref, completion: CompletionResult) -> RoleMailboxMessage` produces a `MessageFamily::AnnounceBack` message whose AnnounceBackBody includes: (a) bundle_id reference, (b) provenance_chain: Vec<ProvenanceLink> (each link: predecessor_message_id + content_hash for tamper-evident chain), (c) completion artifacts, (d) completion_state. Provenance verifier: `verify_chain(messages) -> Result<(), ChainError>` walks the chain from AnnounceBack back to the original DelegateWork, asserts content_hash chain unbroken. Tests: (a) builder rejects missing fields at compile time (typestate); (b) bundle insert recomputes hash and rejects tampered input; (c) compose AnnounceBack, verify chain — passes; (d) tamper with intermediate message body, verify chain — fails with typed ChainError; (e) goldenfile cross-check for canonical JSON hashing.
risk_if_missed: Without typed handoff bundles + provenance chains, delegated work loses its audit trail; announce-back becomes unstructured chat; the folded WP-1-Role-Mailbox-v1 stub's auditable-governance intent silently degrades to copy-paste discipline.
validator_focus: Provenance chain is tamper-evident (content_hash mismatch fails verification); typestate builder prevents missing-field bundles at compile time; bundle table content_hash recomputed on insert (not trusted from caller).

proof.note: Initial MT-183 hash mismatch log is superseded by this passing run.

postgres_test_url_live_evidence_20260525.summary: Handoff bundle and announce-back provenance passed after fixing Postgres timestamp precision in bundle hash input.
residual_risks: Supporting evidence only; Integration Validator verdict remains PENDING.
Focused Postgres/live-kernel commands were run, not a full workspace cargo test.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Tampered-chain test asserts ChainError::ContentHashMismatch is returned, not a soft warning.
Bundle table has a CHECK constraint or trigger asserting content_hash matches canonical JSON (defense-in-depth).
Canonical JSON hashing is deterministic (sorted keys, no trailing whitespace) and proven by golden-fixture.

implementation_proof.summary: MT-183 PENDING -> CLAIMED -> READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Hardened the pre-existing role_mailbox_v1/handoff.rs primitive: (1) added MAX_PROVENANCE_CHAIN_DEPTH=256 + ChainError::DepthExceeded so verify_chain refuses unbounded vectors; (2) added ChainError::DanglingBundleCorrelation + ChainError::MissingBundleReference + new AnnounceBackComposer::verify_announce_back_pairing pairing check; (3) added MailboxError::HashMismatch + RoleMailboxRepository::insert_handoff_bundle / get_handoff_bundle / list_handoff_bundles_for_thread methods which recompute the canonical-JSON sha256 on insert and reject tampered input with a typed error (red_team.minimum_controls #1 + #2 satisfied — caller-supplied hash never trusted). Added owned-files integration-test surface tests/role_mailbox_handoff_tests.rs with 17 always-on pure-Rust adversarial assertions covering (a) serde round-trip, (b) announce-back/bundle correlation, (c) announce-back without prior handoff -> typed error, (d) provenance chain depth limit, (e) dangling correlation, (f) tampered chain link -> ChainError::HashMismatch, (g) empty chain -> ChainError::Empty, (h) missing predecessor -> ChainError::MissingPredecessor, (i) tampered bundle pairing -> ChainError::HashMismatch, (j) Uuid v7 mint site, (k) canonical-hash golden-fixture (order stability + permutation sensitivity + 64-hex-char shape), (l) MessageFamily wrapper round-trip, (m) ChainError + MailboxError Display variants distinct, (n) PgPool-only constructor surface check, (o) transcript pointer round-trip; plus 5 Postgres-gated #[ignore]-gated assertions covering insert-rejects-tampered, get round-trip + verify, chronological list, unknown id -> None, and an end-to-end insert+compose+verify_chain+verify_pairing flow. Wired the new test crate via Cargo.toml [[test]] entry under required-features = ['test-utils'].

proof_commands.command: cargo test --features test-utils --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --test role_mailbox_handoff_tests --test role_mailbox_repo_tests --test role_mailbox_lease_tests --test role_mailbox_v1_tests --test role_mailbox_exporter_tests -- --test-threads=1
proof_commands.result: 5 test crates: handoff 17p/5i, lease 11p/20i, repo 7p/11i, v1 9p/2i, exporter 14p/5i. 58 passing, 43 Postgres-gated, 0 failed. No regression from the repo.rs handoff-method additions or the Cargo.toml [[test]] addition.

red_team_minimum_controls_coverage.control_1_tampered_chain_typed_error: PASS — mt_183_tampered_chain_link_typed_error + mt_183_tampered_bundle_pairing_fails assert ChainError::HashMismatch variant (matches!(..., Err(ChainError::HashMismatch { .. })))
red_team_minimum_controls_coverage.control_2_bundle_insert_recomputes_hash: PASS — RoleMailboxRepository::insert_handoff_bundle recomputes via MailboxHandoffBundleV1::recompute_hash before binding, returns MailboxError::HashMismatch when caller-supplied hash differs; mt_183_insert_handoff_bundle_recomputes_hash_and_rejects_tampered_input asserts the typed error path Postgres-side
red_team_minimum_controls_coverage.control_3_canonical_json_deterministic_g: PASS — mt_183_canonical_hash_is_deterministic_golden_fixture asserts order-stability + 64-hex-char sha256 shape + permutation sensitivity (reversing linked_artifacts changes the hash); serde_json default Map is BTreeMap so keys are sorted by construction (no preserve-order feature in Cargo.toml)

out_of_scope_edits: src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/mod.rs — added MAX_PROVENANCE_CHAIN_DEPTH re-export so the new integration tests can name the bound by symbol. Same crate, same module, additive only.

src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/repo.rs — added insert_handoff_bundle + get_handoff_bundle + list_handoff_bundles_for_thread methods plus MailboxError::HashMismatch variant. The contract explicitly required 'bundle insert recomputes hash and rejects tampered input' so this is in-scope but lives in repo.rs (MT-177 owned-file). Additive only; no behaviour change to MT-177 methods.
src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml — registered the new tests/role_mailbox_handoff_tests.rs binary.
residual_risks: Postgres-side red_team.minimum_controls #2 (CHECK constraint or trigger asserting content_hash matches canonical JSON at the database layer) is NOT implemented; the defence currently runs only in the Rust insert path. A future microtask can add a stored-function trigger that recomputes and rejects at the SQL layer for full defence-in-depth.

Canonical JSON hashing relies on serde_json's BTreeMap default sort. If a future crate-level feature adds preserve-order, the canonical-hash assumption silently breaks; a follow-up MT should pin via Cargo.lock + a clippy-disallowed-methods rule for serde_json preserve_order if a feature gate is ever added.

verify_announce_back_pairing only checks bundle hash + presence — it does NOT cross-check that the AnnounceBack's source_thread matches the bundle's source_thread. A future MT could tighten the pairing by asserting thread parity, message_id parity, and target_role parity at the verification step.

The Postgres-gated assertions are #[ignore]-gated on POSTGRES_TEST_URL per the cluster X.1 Spec-Realism Gate convention; a downstream MT (or the WP integration validator) must run them against a live Postgres to close the loop on insert/get/list paths.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: MailboxHandoffBundleV1 + HandoffBundleBuilder + AnnounceBackComposer landed with MAX_PROVENANCE_CHAIN_DEPTH=256 cap, ChainError typed variants (HashMismatch/DepthExceeded/DanglingBundleCorrelation/MissingPredecessor/Empty), repo-level insert recomputes sha256 and rejects tampered input with MailboxError::HashMismatch. 17 pure-Rust + 5 Postgres-gated tests green (Postgres timestamp-precision regression added 2026-05-25). Canonical JSON hashing relies on serde_json default BTreeMap sort — adequate but fragile if preserve-order feature is ever enabled.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: MailboxHandoffBundleV1 + HandoffBundleBuilder + AnnounceBackComposer landed with MAX_PROVENANCE_CHAIN_DEPTH=256 cap, ChainError typed variants (HashMismatch/DepthExceeded/DanglingBundleCorrelation/MissingPredecessor/Empty), repo-level insert recomputes sha256 and rejects tampered input with MailboxError::HashMismatch. 17 pure-Rust + 5 Postgres-gated tests green (Postgres timestamp-precision regression added 2026-05-25). Canonical JSON hashing relies on serde_json default BTreeMap sort — adequate but fragile if preserve-order feature is ever enabled.
